mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm, which manages the deployment of the operator.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> This command installs the Cluster API components in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to manage your Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  controlPlane:
    machineTemplate:
      spec:
        template:
          spec:
            containers:
            - name: my-container
              image: nginx
EOF

```

> This example creates a simple Cluster resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  controlPlane:
    machineTemplate:
      spec:
        template:
          spec:
            containers:
            - name: my-container
              image: nginx
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API pods running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this may lead to data loss of managed clusters.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster state. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup current cluster state.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: unable to find provider for "aws"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The required provider for AWS is not installed.

**Fix:**
Install the AWS provider using: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api-provider-aws/releases/latest/download/cluster-api-provider-aws.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system and fix any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API controller is unable to communicate with the Kubernetes API server.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your kubeconfig is correctly set up and that the API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for custom providers, added new features for cluster scaling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old v1alpha1 API versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your Cluster resources to the new v1beta1 API.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' showing all Cluster API pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  controlPlane:
    machineTemplate:
      spec:
        template:
          spec:
            containers:
            - name: my-container
              image: nginx
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and have kubectl and Helm installed.
